data.title: Write RED test asserting scan-config-keys.sh produces zero gaps after documentation
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## What
Create `tests/scripts/test-config-keys-documented.sh` — a behavioral test that runs `plugins/dso/scripts/scan-config-keys.sh` and asserts the output is empty (meaning every config key read by code is documented in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md).

## Test approach (Given / When / Then)
- Given a repo with `plugins/dso/scripts/scan-config-keys.sh` present and executable (created by task b1d7-c727)
- When the test invokes `bash plugins/dso/scripts/scan-config-keys.sh \$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)`
- Then the script stdout must be empty (zero lines, or only whitespace) — indicating every scanned config key appears in `plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md`

## Files to Modify
- `tests/scripts/test-config-keys-documented.sh` (create) — the behavioral test
- `.test-index` (modify) — add mapping `plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md: tests/scripts/test-config-keys-documented.sh` AND append RED marker `[test_config_keys_documented]`

## Notes
- Test filename contains 'config-keys' which normalizes to 'configkeys'; source normalized 'configurationreferencemd'. Normalized source is NOT a substring of normalized test — a `.test-index` entry is REQUIRED (included in AC below).
- Test MUST NOT do any stubbing or fixturing — it runs against the real repo state. Gate: real scan, real docs file.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/scripts/test-config-keys-documented.sh
- [ ] .test-index entry maps CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md to this test
  Verify: grep -q 'plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md.*test-config-keys-documented' \$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] .test-index RED marker [test_config_keys_documented] is present
  Verify: grep -q '\[test_config_keys_documented\]' \$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] Test currently fails RED (pre-documentation state produces non-empty gap list)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-config-keys-documented.sh; test \$? -ne 0
